title: Number of Equal Groups
essential_question: How can you model division to find how many equal groups?
strategies: Circle groups of the given size until all counters are used.
Think: total ÷ size of each group = number of groups.
Skip count up to the total.

pretest.stem: 35 counters, 7 in each group. How many groups?
30 counters, 5 in each group. How many groups?
Toby puts 21 books on shelves with 7 per shelf. How many shelves?
Mr. Holden has 32 quarters in stacks of 4. How many stacks?
16 counters, 4 in each group. How many groups?

learn.title: Finding the Number of Groups
learn.content: When you know the total and how many in each group, divide to find the number of groups. 24 ÷ 8 = 3 means 24 items, 8 in each group, makes 3 groups.

learn.title: Model It
learn.content: For 35 ÷ 7: circle groups of 7 from 35 counters. You make 5 circles. So there are 5 groups.

learn.title: Skip Count Up
learn.content: To find 36 ÷ 9: count by 9s → 9, 18, 27, 36. That's 4 counts. So 36 ÷ 9 = 4 groups.

try.stem: 12 counters, 6 in each group. How many groups?
36 counters, 9 in each group. How many groups?

practice_r1.stem: A gardener has 24 plants. He puts 8 in each row. How many rows?
A coach puts 42 balls into bags of 6. How many bags?

practice_r2.stem: A toy store has 56 toy cars in boxes of 8. How many boxes?
A librarian has 45 new books. She fills shelves with 9 each. How many shelves?

practice_r3.stem: A baker bakes 60 muffins. He fills boxes of 6 and sells each box for $4. If he sells all boxes, how much money does he make?
72 students line up in rows of 8. The first 3 rows go inside. How many students are still outside?
practice_r3.explanation: 72 ÷ 8 = 9 rows. 3 rows go in = 3 × 8 = 24. 72 − 24 = 48 still outside.

practice_r3.stem: Emma has 40 stickers. She gives 5 stickers to each friend until she has 10 left. How many friends got stickers?
